vaccination.notes: No vaccine available; prevention through safer sex practices, needle safety, PMTCT

behavioral_prevention.condom_use: Promote consistent and correct use of male/female condoms
behavioral_prevention.safe_injections: Use sterile needles and syringes for all injections
behavioral_prevention.testing: Offer HIV testing and counselling (HTS) to all patients
behavioral_prevention.pmtct: Screen all pregnant women; initiate ART (Option B+)

post_exposure_prophylaxis.pep: TDF/3TC/DTG starter pack within 72 hours of exposure
post_exposure_prophylaxis.indications: Occupational exposure, sexual assault, high-risk unprotected sex

pre_exposure_prophylaxis.injectable: CAB-LA every 2 months, Lenacapavir every 6 months
pre_exposure_prophylaxis.indications: High-risk individuals, discordant couples, sex workers, MSM

first_line.pregnant: Same as adult (DTG safe in pregnancy)
first_line.tb_coinfection: DTG 50mg twice daily when on Rifampicin

alternative_first_line.notes: TAF preferred in patients >50 years, renal impairment, osteoporosis

monitoring.cd4: At initiation and when clinically indicated

infection_control.airborne_precautions: N95 respirator, negative pressure isolation where available
infection_control.cough_hygiene: Cover mouth when coughing, surgical mask for patients
infection_control.ventilation: Ensure adequate ventilation in healthcare settings

tb_preventive_therapy.indications: PLHIV, household contacts of bacteriologically confirmed TB

screening.symptom_checklist: Current cough, night sweats, weight loss, fever
screening.molecular_testing: Xpert MTB/Rif Ultra or Truenat MTB-Rif

drug_sensitive_tb.continuation_phase: HR daily for 4 months (or 10 months for CNS/bone TB)

drug_resistant_tb.mdr_tb: Refer to specialist for individualized regimen

tb_hiv_coinfection.art_timing: Start ART within 2 weeks of TB treatment initiation
tb_hiv_coinfection.dtg_dose: DTG 50mg twice daily when on Rifampicin

adverse_effects.hepatitis: Stop all TB drugs, reintroduce one by one
adverse_effects.management: Start with INH, then R, then E, then Z

treatment_protocols.directly_observed_therapy: DOT by healthcare worker, community member, or family member

vector_control.indoor_residual_spraying: IRS twice yearly in endemic areas

chemoprophylaxis.intermittent_preventive_treatment_pregna: SP 3 tablets at 13 weeks, then 4-weekly until delivery
chemoprophylaxis.seasonal_malaria_chemoprevention: SMC for children in high-risk areas

personal_protection.protective_clothing: Long sleeves at dusk and dawn

surveillance.community_awareness: Educate on early signs and prevention

parenteral_therapy.second_line: Quinine IV loading dose 20mg/kg over 4 hours

supportive_care.hypoglycemia: Dextrose 50% 1ml/kg IV in children, 20-50ml in adults

vaccination.target: All infants, healthcare workers, high-risk groups

screening.high_risk: Screen HIV positive, contacts, healthcare workers

emtct.tdf_prophylaxis: TDF 300mg once daily from 28 weeks to 14 weeks postpartum
emtct.birth_dose: Hepatitis B vaccine within 24 hours of birth

pregnancy.treatment: TDF from 28 weeks if HBV DNA >200,000 IU/mL or HBeAg positive
pregnancy.universal_prophylaxis: TDF for all HBsAg positive if viral load unavailable

screening.antibody_test: Laboratory-based or point-of-care HCV antibody assay
screening.confirmatory: HCV RNA or HCV core antigen for viraemic infection

prevention.safe_tattoos: Regulate tattoo and body piercing practices

high_risk_groups.populations: People who inject drugs, incarcerated persons, HIV coinfection, historical healthcare exposure

monitoring.test_of_cure: Undetectable HCV RNA at 12 weeks post-treatment (SVR12)

water_sanitation.boil_water: Boil drinking water or use safe sources
water_sanitation.safe_food: Cook food thoroughly, avoid raw vegetables
water_sanitation.hand_washing: Wash hands with soap before eating and after toilet

carrier_detection.surveillance: Screen food handlers in outbreak areas

uncomplicated_typhoid.alternative: Norfloxacin 400mg once daily or Cefixime 500-750mg once daily

supportive_care.hydration: IV fluids for dehydration, electrolyte correction
supportive_care.monitoring: Monitor for intestinal perforation and bleeding

water_sanitation.boil_water: Boil drinking water for at least 1 minute
water_sanitation.chlorination: Add 1 tablet of chlorine per 20L of water
water_sanitation.hand_washing: Wash hands with soap before eating and after toilet
water_sanitation.food_safety: Cook food thoroughly, avoid raw food

hygiene_measures.safe_storage: Store water in clean, covered containers
hygiene_measures.community_education: Health education on transmission and prevention

case_management.isolation: Isolate patients in Cholera Treatment Centers (CTC)
case_management.contact_tracing: Trace and provide chemoprophylaxis to household contacts

rehydration.plan_a: ORS at home for no dehydration - 50ml/kg over 4 hours
rehydration.plan_b: ORS at facility for some dehydration - 75ml/kg over 4 hours
rehydration.plan_c: IV Ringer's Lactate for severe dehydration - 100ml/kg over 3 hours
rehydration.monitoring: Monitor urine output, hydration status every 1-2 hours

referral_criteria: Signs of shock (weak pulse, low BP)
No improvement after 6 hours of treatment

vaccination.schedule: Primary series + boosters as per national guidelines

infection_control.droplet_precautions: Surgical mask for patients and healthcare workers
infection_control.hand_hygiene: Alcohol-based hand rub or soap and water

community_measures.isolation: Isolate confirmed cases at home or facility

vaccination.routine: DPT-HepB-Hib at 6, 10, 14 weeks; DPT booster at 18 months
vaccination.wound_prophylaxis: Tetanus toxoid for wounds based on immunization status

supportive_care.nutrition: NG feeding if unable to swallow

treatment_protocols.referral: All cases to designated infectious disease hospital

vaccination.boosters: DPT booster at 18 months, Td at 5 and 10 years

isolation.duration: Until two negative cultures 24 hours apart

contact_management.chemoprophylaxis: Azithromycin 500mg single dose OR Erythromycin 500mg 4 times daily for 7 days

antitoxin.administration: Diphtheria Antitoxin (DAT) as soon as possible

airway_management.intubation: Prepare for tracheostomy if airway compromise

supportive_care.fluid: IV fluids if unable to swallow

behavioral_prevention.condom_use: Promote consistent and correct use of condoms

screening.syndromic_management: Treat based on urethral discharge/vaginal discharge syndromes
screening.contact_tracing: Contact tracing and treatment of partners

first_line.adult: Ceftriaxone 1g IM single dose + Doxycycline 100mg twice daily for 7 days

second_line.adult: Cefixime 800mg single dose + Azithromycin 2g single dose

infection_prevention_control.hand_hygiene: Alcohol-based hand rub or soap and water
infection_prevention_control.ppe: Full PPE for all patient contacts (gown, gloves, mask, goggles, face shield)
infection_prevention_control.isolation: Strict isolation in designated treatment centers

surveillance.case_definition: Acute fever + haemorrhagic symptoms + epidemiological link

community_measures.education: Educate on transmission (bodily fluids, not airborne)
community_measures.avoid_contact: Avoid handling bodies, sick individuals in outbreak areas

supportive_care.fluid_resuscitation: IV fluids (Ringer's Lactate, Normal Saline) for dehydration

monitoring.laboratory: Daily FBC, U+Es, LFTs, Coagulation profile

treatment_protocols.referral: All suspected cases to designated Ebola Treatment Unit

vaccination.vaccine_type: Measles-Rubella (MR) vaccine (contains mumps in some formulations)
vaccination.schedule: MR1 at 9 months, MR2 at 18 months

isolation.duration: Isolate until swelling subsides (about 5 days after onset)

supportive_care.pain_management: Paracetamol or ibuprofen for fever and pain

vaccination.routine: MR1 at 9 months, MR2 at 18 months
vaccination.campaign: Mass vaccination campaigns in outbreak areas

case_isolation.duration: Isolate from 4 days before rash to 4 days after rash appears

outbreak_response.contact_management: Provide Vitamin A to all contacts

vaccination.schedule: MR1 at 9 months, MR2 at 18 months
vaccination.pregnancy: Avoid vaccination in pregnancy; screen for rubella immunity

isolation.duration: Isolate for 7 days after rash onset

pregnancy.management: Refer to obstetric specialist if rubella in pregnancy

vaccination.target: High-risk groups (elderly, chronic disease, healthcare workers)

supportive_care.fever: Paracetamol or ibuprofen for fever and pain

chemoprophylaxis.contacts: All close contacts of confirmed cases

isolation.duration: Respiratory isolation for 24-48 hours after antibiotics

monitoring.neuro: Monitor level of consciousness, pupils 1-2 hourly
monitoring.laboratory: CSF analysis after LP (if no contraindications)

vaccination.schedule: Not in routine Zimbabwe EPI (available privately)

isolation.duration: Isolate until all lesions are crusted (usually 5-7 days after rash)

screening.pregnancy: Rapid point-of-care screening at first antenatal contact

late_syphilis.latent_neurosyphilis_cardiovascular: Benzathine Penicillin 2.4MU IM weekly for 3 weeks

pregnancy.early: Benzathine Penicillin 2.4MU IM weekly for 3 doses
pregnancy.late: Benzathine Penicillin 2.4MU IM weekly for 3 doses

education.awareness: Educate on transmission through water contact

schistosoma_haematobium.special: Visible haematuria or positive urine strip indicates treatment

pregnancy.caution: Do NOT give praziquantel in pregnancy (treat after delivery)

vector_control.protective_clothing: Wear long sleeves, use insect repellent

vector_control.tsetse_traps: Use tsetse traps in endemic areas

surveillance.case_finding: Active case finding in endemic areas (Mashonaland Central, West, Matebeleland North)
surveillance.referral: All suspected cases to designated hospitals

first_line.adult_20_34kg: Fexinidazole loading 1200mg once daily for 4 days, then maintenance 600mg for 6 days
first_line.adult_35kg: Fexinidazole loading 1800mg once daily for 4 days, then maintenance 1200mg for 6 days

children.weight_20kg: Use adult dosing based on weight

alternative_stage_1.drug: Suramin 4-5mg/kg test dose, then 20mg/kg on Day 1,3,7,14,21

treatment_protocols.referral: All cases to Sally Mugabe Central Hospital (Tropical Diseases Unit)

first_line.adult: Doxycycline 100mg twice daily + Streptomycin 1g daily for 14-21 days

alternative.adult: Doxycycline 100mg twice daily + Rifampicin 600mg daily for 6 weeks

screening.blood_pressure: Screen adults at least every 5 years

insulin_therapy.basal: NPH or long-acting analogue 10 units at bedtime
insulin_therapy.premixed: Actraphane or Novomix30 6-12 units twice daily

education.self_management: Educate on insulin administration, blood glucose monitoring, hypoglycemia management

insulin_regimen.basal: 50% of TDDI (NPH twice daily or long-acting analogue once daily)
insulin_regimen.bolus: 50% of TDDI divided into 3 pre-meal doses (rapid or short-acting)

screening.at_risk: Screen patients with hypertension, diabetes, HIV

screening.goitre: Refer for thyroid workup (TSH, FT4, FT3)

medical_management.beta_blocker: Propranolol 40-160mg 3 times daily for symptom control

surgical_management.indications: Failed medical therapy, contraindication to radioiodine, large goitre

thyroid_hormone_replacement.adult: Thyroxine 50-100mcg initially, increase by 25-50mcg every 4 weeks

monitoring.tsh: 4-6 weeks after dose change, then annually

acute_gout.nsaids: Diclofenac 50mg 3 times daily or Ibuprofen 400mg 3 times daily
acute_gout.colchicine: 0.5-1mg up to 6 times daily for 2 days (Specialist)

chronic_gout.allopurinol: Start 100mg once daily, increase every 2 weeks to 300mg

dmards.folate_supplement: 5-10mg 2-3 times weekly (not same day as methotrexate)

sun_protection.avoid_sun: Avoid sun exposure, use broad-brimmed hats and umbrellas

pregnancy.avoid: Sodium Valproate (risk of neural tube defects)

h_pylori_eradication.first_line: Amoxicillin 1g twice daily + Clarithromycin 500mg twice daily + Omeprazole 20mg twice daily for 2 weeks
h_pylori_eradication.alternative: Amoxicillin 1g twice daily + Metronidazole 400mg 3 times daily + Omeprazole 20mg twice daily for 2 weeks

lifestyle.avoid_triggers: Avoid chocolate, colas, coffee, fatty foods, tight clothing

mild.antacids: Magnesium trisilicate 20ml or 2 tablets 4 times daily

nutrition.iron_rich_foods: Encourage meat, fish, dark green vegetables, beans

parenteral_iron.indications: Oral intolerance, non-compliance, approaching term in pregnancy

painful_crisis.analgesia: Morphine for severe pain, Codeine or NSAIDs for mild
painful_crisis.antibiotics: Amoxicillin 500mg 3 times daily for 5 days if infection suspected

hydroxyurea.monitoring: Titrate against HbF, monitor WBC and platelets

physiotherapy.exercise: Strengthening and range of motion exercises

mild_depression.counselling: Counsel, follow up, help deal with stressors

moderate_severe.first_line: Fluoxetine 20-60mg once daily or Citalopram 10-40mg once daily
moderate_severe.alternative: Amitriptyline 50-150mg at night or Imipramine 50-150mg at night

treatment_protocols.duration: At least 6 months after symptom resolution

safety.violent_patient: De-escalation, physical restraint if needed, sedation

duration.first_episode: 12 months after symptom resolution, then taper

skin_care.moisturize: Use aqueous cream or emulsifying ointment

infection_control.hand_hygiene: Strict hand hygiene in neonatal unit

screening.maternal_risk: Screen for maternal sepsis, chorioamnionitis, PROM >18 hours

intrapartum_care.monitoring: Monitor fetal heart rate during labour

resuscitation.thermal_support: Dry and wrap baby, warm environment

name: Preterm Birth
edliz_treatment_path: Kangaroo Mother Care (KMC), Supportive thermal care

antenatal.steroids: Dexamethasone 12mg IM 24 hours apart for 3 days if <34 weeks
antenatal.antenatal_care: Regular antenatal care to detect risk factors

feeding.breast_milk: Express breast milk; give via NG tube if unable to suck

respiratory_support.caffeine: 20mg/kg loading, then 5mg/kg daily for apnoea (<32 weeks)

phototherapy.levels: See EDLIZ Table 3.4 for age/weight-based thresholds

exchange_transfusion.blood: Whole blood less than 5 days old

clean_delivery.sterile_instruments: Use sterile equipment for cord cutting

minimal_handling.care: Minimal handling to avoid triggering spasms

supportive_care.nutrition: NG feeding if unable to suck

antenatal_screening.syphilis_testing: Test all pregnant women at first ANC visit (RPR/TPHA)

name: Neonatal Ophthalmia
edliz_treatment_path: Tetracycline eye ointment, systemic antibiotics if indicated

crede_prophylaxis.prophylaxis: Tetracycline 1% eye ointment instilled in both eyes at birth

antenatal_screening.maternal_sti: Screen and treat maternal gonococcal and chlamydial infection

early_feeding.breastfeeding: Initiate breastfeeding within first hour of life

monitoring.blood_glucose: Monitor for 36-48 hours in high-risk infants

mild.oral_feeds: Consider expressed breast milk or formula

empiric_treatment.if_unable_to_measure: Give glucose via NG tube: 10-20ml/kg breast milk

antenatal_care.bp_monitoring: Monitor blood pressure at every ANC visit
antenatal_care.urine_protein: Check urine for protein at every visit

seizure_control.magnesium_sulphate_loading: 4g IV in 20ml saline over 20 mins + 5g IM in each buttock
seizure_control.magnesium_sulphate_maintenance: 5g IM in alternate buttocks every 4 hours until 24 hours after delivery
seizure_control.monitoring: Patellar reflex, respiratory rate >16/min, urine output >100ml/4hrs

delivery.timing: Urgent delivery within 6 hours of seizure

airway.management: Ensure clear airway, oxygen if available

mild_pih.management: Bed rest at home, weekly visits

imminent_eclampsia.magnesium_sulphate: 4g IV + 5g IM loading, then 5g IM every 4 hours

active_management_3rd_stage.oxytocin: 10 units IM with delivery of anterior shoulder

risk_identification.risk_factors: Identify high-risk patients (grand multipara, prolonged labour, anaemia)

acute_management.oxytocin_infusion: 20 units in 1L Normal Saline at 10-60 drops/min

management.antibiotics: Prophylactic antibiotics if membranes ruptured >24 hours

infection_control.early_detection: Monitor for signs of infection post-delivery

mild_moderate.antibiotics: Amoxicillin 500mg 3 times daily + Metronidazole 400mg 3 times daily

nutrition.dietary_advice: Iron-rich foods (meat, fish, dark green vegetables)

severe_anaemia.investigations: Stool for ova, blood film, FBC, HIV test

early_diagnosis.pregnancy_test: Routine pregnancy test in women of childbearing age with abdominal pain

definitive_management.surgery: Emergency laparotomy with salpingectomy or salpingotomy

vaccination.influenza: Annual influenza vaccine for high-risk groups

inpatient.alternative: Ceftriaxone 1g IV daily/twice daily for 7 days

special_populations.hiv_positive: Amoxicillin 500mg 3 times daily for 7 days, consider PCP

monitoring.review: Review at 48 hours, switch to oral if improving

antibiotics.second_line: Amoxicillin-Clavulanic acid if recent amoxicillin use

early_treatment.aom: Prompt treatment of acute otitis media

do_not_examine_throat.warning: Do NOT examine throat - risk of airway obstruction

airway.monitoring: Watch for signs of obstruction (poor air entry, severe indrawing, restlessness)

chemoprophylaxis.household_contacts: Azithromycin 10-20mg/kg single dose for household contacts

isolation.duration: Isolate until 5 days after antibiotics started

referral.infants: Infants <6 months with severe symptoms to tertiary care

education.parents: Educate parents on choking hazards (small objects, nuts, beads)

bronchoscopy.procedure: Emergency bronchoscopy for foreign body removal

antibiotics.indication: If secondary infection with fast breathing

fire_safety.education: Educate on burn prevention (hot liquids, fire, electricity)

large_burns.monitoring: Monitor input/output, pulse, BP, urine output

referral.criteria: Adults >10% BSA, children >8-10% BSA, burns of special regions

analgesia.opioids: Morphine or pethidine for severe pain

vaccination.hpv_vaccine: HPV1 at 10 years, HPV2 at 11 years (routine)

referral.oncology: Refer for surgery, radiotherapy, or chemotherapy
Refer for surgery, chemotherapy, radiotherapy, or hormonal therapy
Refer for hormonal therapy, radiotherapy, or chemotherapy

screening.fbc: Full blood count for suspected cases

referral.oncology: Refer for chemotherapy and specialist management
Refer for chemotherapy, radiotherapy, or surgery
Refer for surgery, chemotherapy, or palliative care
Refer for surgery, chemotherapy, or palliative care

hepatitis_management.treat_hepatitis_b_c: Treat chronic hepatitis B and C

referral.oncology: Refer for palliative care, targeted therapy, or specialist management
Refer for surgery, radiotherapy, or chemotherapy

biopsy.indication: Biopsy any ulcer that does not heal in 14 days

infection_prevention_control.hand_hygiene: Alcohol-based hand rub or soap and water
infection_prevention_control.ppe: Full PPE for all patient contacts

surveillance.case_definition: Acute fever + haemorrhagic symptoms + epidemiological link

treatment_protocols.referral: All suspected cases to designated treatment facility

lifestyle.calcium_diet: Ensure adequate dietary calcium (kapenta, dairy, green vegetables)

antibiotic_stewardship.reserve_only: Reserve antibiotics only as last resort

culture_guided.susceptibility: Treat based on culture and sensitivity results

referral.criteria: All MDR cases to tertiary care

cotrimoxazole_prophylaxis.adults: 960mg once daily if CD4 <350 or WHO stage 3/4
cotrimoxazole_prophylaxis.children: Weight-based dosing for all HIV+ children

art.initiation: Start ART as soon as possible

cryptococcal_meningitis.duration: Induction 2 weeks, consolidation 8 weeks, maintenance 1 year

surgical.abscess: Incision and drainage if abscess present

prophylaxis.dental: Amoxicillin 3g PO 1 hour before dental procedures (high-risk patients)

infection_control.ppe: Full PPE for all patient contacts

treatment_protocols.referral: All suspected cases to designated treatment facility

referral.specialist: Refer for culture-guided therapy and specialist management

active_bleeding.ffp: Fresh Frozen Plasma if bleeding is active

medication_management.opioid_caution: Use opioids cautiously, especially in elderly
medication_management.monitoring: Monitor respiratory rate after opioid administration

feeding.slow_feeding: Slow advancement of feeds in preterm infants

referral.surgery: Refer for specialist diagnosis and possible surgery

screening.at_risk: Monitor calcium in neonates, renal failure, post-thyroidectomy

emergency.chest_tube: Chest tube insertion for significant pneumothorax

caffeine_prophylaxis.caffeine_citrate: 20mg/kg loading dose, then 5mg/kg daily for preterm <32 weeks

monitoring.apnoea_monitor: Use apnoea monitor or pulse oximeter
monitoring.exclude_causes: Rule out infection, hypoxia, hypoglycaemia, anaemia

airway_management.monitoring: Monitor for signs of airway obstruction

aspiration_prevention.positioning: Position patients appropriately to prevent aspiration

streptococcal_treatment.sore_throat: Treat streptococcal sore throat with penicillin

systemic.fluconazole: 200mg twice daily for 7-14 days (for oesophageal candidiasis)

hygiene.hand_washing: Wash hands with soap after toilet
Wash hands with soap after toilet

mental_health.referral: Refer for psychological support and counseling

animal_control.carcass_disposal: Burn or deep bury animal carcasses

surveillance.no_lab_specimens: Do NOT take laboratory specimens (treat clinically)

mild_cases.regimen: Doxycycline 200mg first dose, then 100mg once daily

pulmonary_anthrax.treatment: Refer to designated Infectious Disease Hospital

wound_care.incision: Do NOT incise or drain the eschar

flea_control.insecticides: Apply insecticides to areas with high flea populations

human_pre_exposure.schedule: Rabies vaccine 0.1ml ID on Day 0 and Day 7
human_pre_exposure.booster: Every 2-3 years if serological testing not available

community_education.bite_management: Wash wound immediately with soap and water
community_education.avoid_wildlife: Avoid handling stray or wild animals
community_education.reporting: Report animal bites to health facility

wound_care.immediate: Thoroughly wash with soap and water or povidone-iodine
wound_care.do_not: Do not suture wound unless absolutely necessary

category_iii.immunoglobulin: Human RIG 10 IU/kg infiltrated at wound site

monitoring.vaccine_course: Complete full vaccine course regardless of time since exposure

awareness.early_treatment: Seek treatment early if symptoms appear

treatment.adult: Doxycycline 200mg first dose, then 100mg once daily

referral.criteria: If no improvement after 7 days, refer for further evaluation

vaccination.routine: OPV1, OPV2, OPV3 at 6, 10, 14 weeks; IPV at 9 and 14 weeks

surveillance.case_definition: Acute flaccid paralysis in a child < 15 years
surveillance.stool_collection: Collect two stool samples 24 hours apart, within 14 days of paralysis

contact_tracing.investigation: Investigate all AFP cases within 48 hours

supportive_care.positioning: Position patient to avoid pressure ulcers

management.nutrition: High-calorie diet via NG tube if needed

vaccination.schedule: Not currently in Zimbabwe EPI schedule

water_sanitation.hand_washing: Wash hands before eating and after toilet
Wash hands before eating and after toilet

pregnancy.high_risk: High risk of mortality in pregnancy - refer immediately
pregnancy.monitoring: Monitor for signs of acute liver failure

vaccination.target: Travelers to endemic areas, populations in risk zones

surveillance.case_definition: Acute fever + jaundice + haemorrhagic symptoms + epidemiological link

treatment_protocols.referral: All suspected cases to designated treatment facility

nutrition.malnutrition_prevention: Prevent and treat malnutrition in children

treatment_protocols.referral: All cases to maxillofacial surgeons at central hospital

hygiene.hand_washing: Wash hands with soap after toilet
hygiene.breastfeeding: Promote exclusive breastfeeding for first 6 months

education.community: Educate on diarrhoea prevention and ORT

rehydration.plan_a: ORS at home for no dehydration
rehydration.plan_b: ORS at facility for some dehydration
